text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. One area of technology which has been in the news a lot recently
zh: 我是尼尔。我是贝丝。最近新闻里经常出现的一个科技领域,

text: is robotics – the design and building of robots.
zh: 就是机器人技术——机器人的设计与制造。

text: Humanoid robots, meaning robots which appear and behave like humans,
zh: 人形机器人,也就是外形和行为都像人类的机器人,

text: are being built to help us do everything from washing the dishes to babysitting the kids.
zh: 正在被研发出来,帮我们做各种事情,从洗碗到照看孩子。

text: But not everyone feels comfortable with the idea of inviting a human -like robot into their home.
zh: 但并非每个人都能接受把这种类人机器人请进家门的想法。

text: Neil, how would you feel about living with a robot?
zh: 尼尔,你会怎么看和机器人一起生活?

text: I really don't want to live with a robot. I have enough people in my house.
zh: 我真的不想和机器人一起生活。我家里的人已经够多了。

text: With advances in AI, robots are becoming smart enough to develop meaningful relationships with with humans.
zh: 随着人工智能的发展,机器人正变得足够聪明,能够与人类建立起有意义的关系。

text: There are reports of people telling their secrets to robot therapists and even falling in love with their robot friend.
zh: 有报道称,有人向机器人心理治疗师倾诉秘密,甚至爱上了自己的机器人朋友。

text: In this episode, we'll meet a home companion robot called Abby,
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将认识一个名叫Abby的家庭陪伴机器人,

text: who's making friends with the elderly residents of Melbourne in Australia.
zh: 她正在和澳大利亚墨尔本的老年居民们交朋友。

text: And as usual, we'll learn some useful new vocabulary, all of which you'll find over on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 像往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新词汇,这些词汇都可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: First, I have a question for you, Beth. Because it's important people feel safe around robots,
zh: 首先,我有个问题要问你,贝丝。因为让人们在机器人面前感到安全很重要,

text: engineers designed them to look like the friendly characters seen in animated movies by studios like Pixar and Dreamworks.
zh: 工程师们把机器人设计得像皮克斯和梦工厂等动画公司电影中那些讨人喜欢的角色。

text: So, what is the name of Pixar's friendly robot
zh: 那么,皮克斯那个在2805年的无人地球上捡垃圾的友好机器人,

text: who picks up garbage on an uninhabited planet Earth in the year 2805?
zh: 叫什么名字?

text: Is it a Ros, b Wally, or c R2 -D2?
zh: 是a. Ros,b. Wally,还是c. R2-D2?

text: I am pretty certain that the answer is b Wally. Well, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 我相当确定答案是b,Wally。好,我们稍后会在节目中揭晓答案。

text: For five months during the Covid pandemic, Australian engineer Grace Brown had no human contact.
zh: 在新冠疫情期间的五个月里,澳大利亚工程师格雷丝·布朗没有任何人际接触。

text: She used this time to build a home companion robot, Abby,
zh: 她利用这段时间制作了一个家庭陪伴机器人Abby,

text: with the idea it would provide companionship to other people suffering loneliness. Now Grace runs her own company,
zh: 初衷是希望它能为其他忍受孤独的人提供陪伴。如今,格雷丝经营着自己的公司,

text: Andromeda, building robots specially designed as company for elderly residents in care homes who don't get many visitors.
zh: Andromeda,专门制造机器人,为养老院里很少有人探望的老年居民提供陪伴。

text: Here she explains more about Abby to BBC World Service programme, The Conversation.
zh: 她在BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》中详细介绍了Abby。

text: Abby, who's the humanoid robot that we build at Andromeda.
zh: Abby,是我们在Andromeda公司打造的人形机器人。

text: People don't expect humanoid robots to have like so much personality.
zh: 人们通常不会想到人形机器人会有这么丰富的个性。

text: She's very, very sassy. She's very inquisitive and curious.
zh: 她非常、非常爽快机灵,充满好奇心,爱刨根问底。

text: And I think when people meet her, they're always kind of taken aback by, oh, she's like got a mind of her own.
zh: 我觉得人们见到她时,总会有点吃惊,心想,哦,她好像有自己的主见。

text: When people meet Abby, they are surprised by her sassy personality.
zh: 人们见到Abby时,都会对她那种俏皮自信的个性感到惊讶。

text: The adjective sassy means bold, confident and cheeky.
zh: 形容词sassy意思是大胆、自信又带点调皮。

text: Some people are taken aback, meaning they are shocked or surprised by the things Abby Abby says.
zh: 有些人会感到taken aback,意思是被Abby说的话惊到或吓一跳。

text: She really seems to be interested and to take care of the people she meets.
zh: 她似乎真心对遇到的人感兴趣,并且很关心他们。

text: Abby can also express opinions. And Grace says she has a mind of her own –
zh: Abby还能表达自己的观点。格雷丝说她“有自己的主见”——

text: a phrase used when a non -living object, like a robot, seems to be behaving or acting independently of human control.
zh: 这个短语用来形容像机器人这样的非生命体,表现得仿佛不受人类控制、自行其是。

text: Unlike the unpredictable and violent robots, which are sometimes portrayed in sci -fi movies, movies.
zh: 不同于科幻电影中有时描绘的那种难以预测、充满暴力的机器人,

text: Abby is much more approachable and friendly, even
zh: Abby要平易近人、友善得多,甚至

text: funny. She can blow bubbles from her hand.
zh: 很有趣。她能用手吹出泡泡。

text: Her softer design was a conscious decision by
zh: 她那种较为柔和的设计,是格雷丝深思熟虑后的选择,

text: Grace, who wanted children and elderly people to interact with Abby naturally and without fear,
zh: 她希望孩子和老年人都能自然而然、毫无畏惧地与Abby互动,

text: as she explained to BBC World Service's The Conversation.
zh: 正如她在BBC世界服务《The Conversation》节目中所解释的那样。

text: I originally modelled her off the size of like a young child, so like a six, seven -year -old tall.
zh: 我一开始是按照一个小孩子的大小来设计她的,大概是六七岁孩子的身高。

text: She's got a whole splash of different colours. She's got, you know, large expressive eyes. So that was, I was very much trying
zh: 她身上有各种缤纷的色彩,还有一双大大的、富有表现力的眼睛。所以我当时其实是想

text: to replicate the kind of approachableness and the endearingness of like these different like Pixar characters that you'd watch in all of these films.
zh: 复制出皮克斯电影里那些角色的那种亲切感和惹人喜爱的特质。

text: Abby looks like a friendly character from a Pixar film.
zh: Abby看起来就像皮克斯电影里那种讨人喜欢的角色。

text: She's the size of a six or seven -year -old child and decorated with a splash of colour,
zh: 她的身高相当于六七岁的孩子,身上还点缀着一抹色彩,

text: a phrase used when a bit of colour is added to brighten up something which is dull or unexciting.
zh: a splash of colour这个短语,用来形容为某个原本单调乏味的东西添上一点色彩、增添亮色。

text: Abi also has large expressive eyes.
zh: Abby还有一双富有表现力的大眼睛。

text: If you describe something as expressive, you mean it shows what someone thinks or feels.
zh: 如果你说某样东西expressive,意思是它能表现出一个人的想法或感受。

text: Elderly residents who regularly chat with Abi say the robot companion brings them joy and a sense of community.
zh: 经常和Abby聊天的老年居民表示,这个机器人伙伴给他们带来了快乐和一种归属感。

text: Well, it is good to hear the positive side of technology,
zh: 听到科技带来积极的一面,真是件好事,

text: which can still seem a bit strange or frightening. And I would like to see Abi blowing bubbles from her hand.
zh: 毕竟科技有时候还是会让人觉得有点陌生甚至可怕。我倒挺想亲眼看看Abby用手吹泡泡的样子。

text: Now, isn't it time you revealed the answer to your question, Neil?
zh: 尼尔,是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: I asked you for the name of Pixar's friendly robot who picks up garbage on an uninhabited planet Earth in the year 2805.
zh: 我问的是,皮克斯那个在2805年的无人地球上捡垃圾的友好机器人叫什么名字。

text: And I said it was WALL -E. And you were correct.
zh: 我说答案是WALL-E。你答对了。

text: Yes. That is the right answer. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with
zh: 没错,这就是正确答案。好,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,先从

text: humanoid, an adjective meaning to have the appearance and behaviour of a human.
zh: humanoid开始,这个形容词的意思是具有人类的外貌和行为。

text: Someone who is sassy is bold, confident and cheeky.
zh: 一个人如果是sassy,就是说他大胆、自信又带点调皮。

text: If you are taken aback by something, you are shocked or surprised by it.
zh: 如果你对某件事taken aback,意思是你被它震惊或吓了一跳。

text: If you say an object has a mind of its own, you mean that it seems to be acting independently, without human control.
zh: 如果你说某个物体有a mind of its own,意思是它似乎在不受人类控制的情况下自行其是。

text: The phrase a splash of colour is used when a bit of colour is added to brighten something which is dull or unexciting.
zh: a splash of colour这个短语,用来形容给原本单调乏味的东西添上一抹亮色。

text: And finally, the adjective expressive means showing what someone thinks or feels.
zh: 最后,形容词expressive的意思是能表现出一个人的想法或感受。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. If you've enjoyed this episode,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。如果你喜欢这一期节目,

text: then head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com where you'll find a quiz and worksheet to practice the vocabulary you've
zh: 就请前往我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,那里有测验和练习题,帮你巩固所学的词汇。

text: learnt. See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们很快再见,现在,先说再见。
